title: Framework for Regulated Entities under AI/ML-Based Investment Recommendations
summary: SEBI introduces a comprehensive framework requiring all investment advisors and research analysts using AI/ML tools to maintain explainability logs, conduct periodic audits, and ensure client disclosures about algorithm-based recommendations.
botSummary: SEBI now requires anyone giving AI-powered stock tips to: 1) Keep records of how their AI made decisions, 2) Get regular audits, and 3) Tell clients when advice comes from an algorithm. This protects retail investors from 'black box' recommendations.

title: Revision of Closing Auction Session Timings for Equity Segment
summary: NSE revises the closing auction session timing from 3:40 PM to 4:00 PM to 3:45 PM to 4:00 PM, effective February 1, 2026. The pre-open session remains unchanged.
botSummary: The 'closing auction' is how NSE determines the final price of stocks each day. It's now 5 minutes shorter (3:45-4:00 PM instead of 3:40-4:00 PM). This means less time for big institutions to place large orders that set the closing price.

title: Enhanced Disclosures for Mutual Funds with International Exposure
summary: Mutual funds with more than 20% international exposure must now provide quarterly reports on currency hedging costs, geopolitical risk assessments, and detailed country-wise allocation breakdowns.
botSummary: If your mutual fund invests abroad, you'll now get clearer reports every 3 months showing: how much currency fluctuations cost you, which countries your money is in, and what global risks might affect your returns. Better transparency for global investors.

title: Introduction of T+0 Settlement Cycle for Select Securities
summary: BSE announces the introduction of T+0 (same-day) settlement for top 500 securities by market capitalization, effective March 2026. This reduces counterparty risk and improves capital efficiency.
botSummary: Currently when you sell a stock, you get money in T+1 (next day). For top 500 stocks, BSE will offer T+0, you get your money the SAME day you sell. This means faster access to funds and less risk of the buyer defaulting.

title: Guidelines on Investor Grievance Redressal Through ODR Platform
summary: SEBI mandates all market intermediaries to integrate with the Online Dispute Resolution (ODR) platform. Disputes under ₹50 lakhs must be resolved within 60 days through this mechanism.
botSummary: Got a complaint against your broker or mutual fund? SEBI now requires all financial companies to use an online dispute system. If your issue is under ₹50 lakhs, they MUST resolve it within 60 days, no more endless delays.

title: Margin Requirements for Intraday Derivative Positions
summary: NSE revises peak margin requirements for intraday derivative positions, introducing a tiered system based on volatility bands. High-volatility periods will require 125% of standard margins.
botSummary: Trading F&O intraday? When markets get volatile, you'll now need 25% extra margin. NSE will track volatility in real-time and adjust margins accordingly. This prevents over-leveraging during wild market swings.

title: Cybersecurity Framework for Stock Brokers and Depository Participants
summary: SEBI issues comprehensive cybersecurity guidelines requiring brokers to implement zero-trust architecture, conduct quarterly penetration testing, and maintain 24/7 Security Operations Centers.
botSummary: Your broker must now have bank-level cybersecurity: assume no one is trusted by default (zero-trust), test their systems for hacks every 3 months, and monitor for attacks 24/7. Your trading account just got more secure.

title: New Framework for Small and Medium REITs
summary: BSE introduces a simplified listing framework for SM-REITs with asset values between ₹50 crore and ₹500 crore, enabling fractional ownership of commercial real estate for retail investors.
botSummary: Want to own a piece of a commercial building without crores? BSE now allows smaller real estate investment trusts (₹50-500 crore) to list. You can invest small amounts and earn rent from offices, malls, or warehouses.
